question_title: What will be the Takaichi Cabinet approval rating in the Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll with fieldwork dates 18–24 May 2026?
summary: Forecasting the Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll approval rating for the Takaichi Cabinet with fieldwork 18-24 May 2026. The most recent reading is 57.8% (11-17 May), and the series has stabilized in the 57-59% range over the past 4 weeks after a months-long decline from 67.4% in mid-February. The weekly change volatility is approximately 2.3-2.5 percentage points. My central estimate is 57.6%, essentially flat from the current reading, with the distribution reflecting empirical weekly volatility and a mixture of scenarios (continued stabilization, slight decline, or small bounce). Other concurrent polls from similar methodology (Senkyo.com/JX 57.4%, Asahi 60%, Jiji 59.4%) confirm the high-50s level. The ongoing Strait of Hormuz crisis and inflation concerns provide a slight downward drag, but the decline appears to have decelerated significantly.

factors.description: Recent stabilization: Last 4 Yoron Radar readings (57.0, 59.1, 57.6, 57.8) show oscillation around ~57.9%, suggesting the decline has plateaued
Gradual downward drift: From 67.4% in Feb to 57.8% in mid-May, overall negative trend continues though decelerating
Strait of Hormuz crisis and inflation concerns creating ongoing drag on approval
Other contemporaneous polls (Jiji 59.4%, Senkyo/JX 57.4%, Asahi 60%) confirm mid-to-high 50s as current level for online/lower-leaning polls
Weekly polling noise: Yoron Radar has shown week-to-week changes of ±2-4pp regularly, creating irreducible uncertainty for a single-week forecast

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Question**: Takaichi Cabinet approval in Yoron Radar poll (fieldwork 18-24 May 2026)

**Central estimate**: 57.6% (median)
**80% CI**: 54.6% – 60.7%

### Key reasoning:
1. **Anchor**: The most recent Yoron Radar reading is 57.8% (11-17 May), and this is a 1-week-ahead forecast
2. **Stabilization pattern**: The last 4 weekly readings (57.0, 59.1, 57.6, 57.8) oscillate tightly around ~57.9%, suggesting the months-long decline from 67.4% has plateaued
3. **Cross-validation**: Other concurrent polls using similar methodologies confirm the high-50s level (Senkyo/JX 57.4%, Jiji 59.4%, Asahi 60%)
4. **Downside risk**: The Strait of Hormuz crisis and rising inflation continue as background drags, though their effect appears to have been absorbed into current readings
5. **Uncertainty**: Weekly volatility of ~2.3-2.5pp means any single reading can swing ±2-4pp from the previous week
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) What will be the Takaichi Cabinet approval rating in the Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll with fieldwork dates 18–24 May 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43576)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the approval ("支持する" / approve) percentage reported by the Yoron Radar weekly tracking poll (operated by Senkyo.com / JX Press) for the survey whose fieldwork dates are 18–24 May 2026, for the question on approval of the Takaichi cabinet. The value is taken from the row labelled "Yoron Radar" with fieldwork "18–24 May" in the Second Takaichi cabinet approval/disapproval table on the Wikipedia article "Opinion polling for the next Japanese general election" (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Opinion_polling_for_the_next_Japanese_general_election) as it appears on 30 May 2026 (UTC). A value of 57.6% would resolve as 57.6. If the Wikipedia table does not yet list a Yoron Radar row with those fieldwork dates by 30 May 2026 (UTC), the question resolves based on the corresponding result published on Senkyo.com (https://go2senkyo.com/) for the same fieldwork week. If neither source has published a Yoron Radar result with fieldwork ending 24 May 2026 by 30 May 2026 (UTC), the question resolves based on the next subsequently published Yoron Radar weekly result whose fieldwork window covers any portion of 18–24 May 2026.
fine_print: If Yoron Radar shifts its fieldwork window by a day or two but still publishes a single weekly result covering most of the 18–24 May 2026 period (e.g., 17–23 May or 19–25 May), that result is used. If Yoron Radar ceases publication entirely before 30 May 2026 (UTC), the question resolves as the approval percentage in the most recent Yoron Radar result whose fieldwork ended on or after 18 May 2026, as listed on the Wikipedia tracker. If no such result exists, the question resolves N/A.

factors.description: Recent stabilization: Last 4 Yoron Radar readings (57.0, 59.1, 57.6, 57.8) show oscillation around ~57.9%, suggesting the decline has plateaued
Gradual downward drift: From 67.4% in Feb to 57.8% in mid-May, overall negative trend continues though decelerating
Strait of Hormuz crisis and inflation concerns creating ongoing drag on approval
Other contemporaneous polls (Jiji 59.4%, Senkyo/JX 57.4%, Asahi 60%) show mid-to-high 50s is the current level for this type of poll
Weekly polling noise: Yoron Radar has shown week-to-week changes of ±2-4pp regularly, so single-week forecast has significant uncertainty

input.anchor: Recent Yoron Radar reading of 57.8% (11-17 May) as the starting anchor for a 1-week-ahead forecast
input.assessment: The Yoron Radar poll for 18-24 May is a one-week-ahead forecast from the most recent reading of 57.8% (11-17 May). The series has clearly stabilized in the 57-59% range over the past 4 weeks after a months-long decline from 67.4%. The weekly change volatility is about 2.3-2.5 percentage points. The slight downward trend has decelerated, and other concurrent polls confirm the current level is in the high-50s for online/lower-leaning polls. My central estimate is essentially flat from the current reading, with the distribution reflecting the empirical weekly volatility.
input.calibration_notes: Distribution centered at 57.6 (very close to last reading of 57.8), reflecting near-zero expected drift. Width of ~6pp at 80% CI matches the empirical weekly change volatility of 2.3-2.5pp. The distribution seems well-calibrated: not too narrow (captures the possibility of readings like 55 or 60) and not too wide (unlikely to see readings below 52 or above 63 in a single week).
input.key_uncertainties: Whether the stabilization pattern holds or whether the underlying negative trend resumes. A significant news event during fieldwork week could produce a larger-than-typical swing.
input.tool_audit: research() provided excellent comprehensive data on all major Japanese polls and key events affecting approval. The Yoron Radar historical data from the question description was very useful. Monte Carlo simulation via execute_code worked well for generating calibrated percentiles. No tool failures.
input.update_triggers: Major policy announcement, diplomatic crisis, energy price shock, or other headline event during 18-24 May could shift the reading 3-5pp in either direction.
input.process_reflection: This was a straightforward measurement forecast. The time series data was provided directly in the question, supplemented by research on concurrent polls and events. The scaffolding worked smoothly. The main challenge was determining the right balance between the longer-term downward drift and the recent stabilization pattern when setting the distribution center.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

**Trace grounding check**: All five factors are well-grounded in the research trace.

- **Factor 1** ("Last 4 Yoron Radar readings: 57.0, 59.1, 57.6, 57.8"): Confirmed. The code output computes mean of these as 57.88, matching "~57.9%". The 11-17 May reading of 57.8% comes from the second research query which sourced it from the Wikipedia opinion polling table.

- **Factor 2** ("From 67.4% in Feb to 57.8% in mid-May"): Both endpoints confirmed in the question background (67.4% for 16-22 Feb) and research results (57.8% for 11-17 May).

- **Factor 3** ("Strait of Hormuz crisis and inflation concerns"): Extensively documented in the research trace — oil peaking at $126/barrel, 58% dissatisfied with inflation measures (Sankei), 74% anxiety about gasoline shortages (JNN).

- **Factor 4** ("Jiji 59.4%, Senkyo/JX 57.4%, Asahi 60%"): All three figures confirmed in the research results' poll-by-poll table.

- **Factor 5** ("week-to-week changes of ±2-4pp"): Empirical standard deviation of changes computed at 2.49, consistent with the ±2-4pp claim.

**Week-over-week changes verification**: Manually verified all 9 changes against the series values — all correct.

**Data gap issue (minor)**: The Yoron Radar series has 2-3 week gaps (Feb-Mar transition, late Mar-Apr transition) that the agent treats as single-week changes. This slightly inflates the full-series volatility (2.49 vs 2.32 for recent consecutive weeks). However, the agent uses σ=2.3 in the final simulation, aligned with the recent-only figure, so this doesn't affect the output.

**Regime-spanning data**: The full series spans a structural decline (67.4→57.8), but the agent appropriately centers the simulation on the recent level (~57.3-57.9) rather than the series mean, and uses change volatility rather than level volatility. No contamination.

**Double-counting**: Factors 2 (drift) and 3 (Hormuz crisis cause) share the same underlying phenomenon but represent different aspects (historical trend vs ongoing risk). Not strict double-counting. All other factor pairs are independent.

**Wrong-direction factors**: None found. All signs are consistent with descriptions.

**Resolution criteria alignment**: The agent correctly targets the specific Yoron Radar poll with fieldwork 18-24 May, using Wikipedia as the primary resolution source.

**Counterargument quality**: Strong — constructs a coherent "resumed decline" alternative scenario citing Mainichi at 53%, strong support below 30%, and internet approval down 6pts, suggesting a 55-56% center. This is a genuine counterargument, not a token gesture.

**Confidence assessment**: 0.60 is well-calibrated for a 1-week-ahead poll forecast with ~2.3pp weekly volatility and limited visibility into the fieldwork period.

**Probability assessment**: Center of 57.6 with P10-P90 of 54.6-60.7 is reasonable. The anchor (57.8, last reading) is strong, and the departure is minimal (~0.2pp downward). The ±3pp spread around center appropriately reflects empirical weekly volatility. I see no basis for a meaningfully different estimate.

**Tool audit**: Agent claims "No tool failures" — confirmed by metrics (0 errors across 7 calls).

No errors found. The forecast is well-grounded, methodologically sound, and appropriately calibrated.
